affected_groups: Teachers at closing schools facing redeployment or severance
Teachers at receiving/consolidated sites facing increased workload
Future policymakers constrained or unconstrained by binding funding commitments
content.decision_question: Employment security and fair working conditions versus administrative cost-efficiency in implementing closures
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and partly known: the scenario's own political-risk analysis already flags that compensatory measures depend on multi-year funding vulnerable to budget cycles, and TEIP's 15-year record is cited as evidence that compensatory support has not resolved equity gaps in comparable contexts; what remains genuinely unresolved is whether a specific binding, ring-fenced legal mechanism would function differently, which is a policy-design question rather than a purely empirical one
content.tension: Closure-as-default treats severance, redeployment and compensatory funding as mere line-item costs without any binding mechanism protecting teacher job continuity, workload, or ring-fenced multi-year funding against future budget cuts.
content.title: Decision tension A16: S4
value_poles: Employment security and fair working conditions
